items.title: Overheated chemical tank may have cracked, California officials say
items.summary_auto: Orange County Fire Authority Capt. Wayhowe Huang said officials will be continuing to evaluate the tank Sunday after emergency crews spotted the potential crack overnight. As of Sunday morning, he said it does not appear that any of the highly volatile chemicals in the tank have leaked.

items.title: 'We got to the finish line': 9-year-old P.E.I. boy enjoys homecoming after heart transplant
items.summary_auto: Nine-year-old Maverick Bishop returned home on Saturday after spending 20 months in Toronto waiting for, then recovering from, a heart transplant.

items.title: Helmet worn by Canadian F1 great Gilles Villeneuve sells for record $1.25M US
items.summary_auto: Gilles Villeneuve's helmet from his final Formula One race in 1982 has sold for a record $1.25 million US, according to a motorsports memorabilia collector.

items.title: Bridgetown, N.S. street stung by internet outage that's lasted more than a week
items.summary_auto: Some Bell Canada customers in Bridgetown, N.S., have been without internet service since May 14 despite repeated calls for the service to be reconnected.

items.title: Camping in B.C. this summer? Here are the places visitors have liked ... and haven't
items.summary_auto: CBC News requested visitor satisfaction surveys and crunched the numbers on what people like and don’t like about every B.C. Parks campground to help campers, and potential campers, know before they go.

items.title: Details emerge about potential Iran deal as Trump says not to rush
items.summary_auto: The potential deal will include Iran's commitment that it won’t pursue a nuclear weapon, and Tehran agreed to give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, according to two regional officials.

items.title: Volunteer firefighter dies while battling wildfire in Nova Scotia
items.summary_auto: A volunteer firefighter with the Bridgetown Volunteer Fire Department died Thursday evening while fighting a wildfire in Moschelle, Annapolis County.

items.title: Vancouver isn’t shipping unhoused people to Prince George for World Cup, officials say. But rumours persist
items.summary_auto: The belief that unhoused people are being paid and provided buses to travel from Vancouver to other communities across British Columbia ahead of the World Cup has taken such a hold in Prince George that the city and multiple councillors have officially come forward to dispel the rumour.

items.title: Dean Penney found guilty of murdering his estranged wife
items.summary_auto: A 12-person jury has found Dean Penney guilty of first-degree murder in relation to the disappearance of his estranged wife, Jennifer Hillier-Penney, in 2016.

items.title: Here's how Alberta zoos are protecting birds from avian flu
items.summary_auto: Zoos and wildlife facilities in Alberta are taking steps to protect their birds from avian flu. Outbreaks on commercial poultry operations in Alberta have been detected east of Edmonton and near Ribstone Creek in east-central Alberta on May 11, and northeast of Drumheller on May 16.

items.title: Russia uses hypersonic Oreshnik missile in mass attack on Kyiv
items.summary_auto: Russia first used the multiple-warhead Oreshnik on the Ukrainian city of Dnipro in November 2024. It was used a second time in January in the western Lviv region.

items.title: Building under construction in the Philippines collapses, with 24 surviving and 21 missing
items.summary_auto: Rescuers including firefighters, police and disaster-response teams used their hands and sniffer dogs in a dangerous scramble to search for the trapped survivors.

items.title: Rescuers race to find survivors after deadly Chinese coal mine blast
items.summary_auto: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for a thorough investigation and accountability of those responsible, after the deadly gas explosion happened Friday evening at the Liushenyu coal mine in the province of Shanxi.

items.title: Suicide bombing near a railway track in southwest Pakistan kills at least 23 people
items.summary_auto: The attack happened in an area where security forces are usually stationed, badly damaging several nearby buildings and smashing vehicles parked along the road, according to witnesses and images circulating on social media.

items.title: Do old buildings give you the creeps? New research could explain haunted feelings
items.summary_auto: Rodney Schmaltz, a psychology professor at MacEwan University in Edmonton, is interested in why some people believe in the paranormal. He and his colleagues studied the effect of infrasound on the body and mind.

items.title: Over $5M in donations flowed in after the Lapu-Lapu Day attack. Here's where it went
items.summary_auto: Several charities and 29 GoFundMe campaigns raised more than $5.5 million for those affected by the Lapu-Lapu Day Festival attack. As some survivors criticize a lack of consultation and ongoing support, one charity expert says direct cash transfers to victims is a proven strategy to maximize impact after a tragedy.

items.title: What centuries-old whaler logs can tell us about why bowheads struggle today
items.summary_auto: Tens of thousands of old logbooks from the 18th and 19th centuries reveal the scope and scale of how industrial whaling nearly wiped out bowhead whales. But they also show why some modern-day populations are surviving better than others.

items.title: E-bike battery fires are a hazard across Canada. These companies are pitching solutions
items.summary_auto: Fires and explosions from lithium batteries for e-bikes have led to millions in damage and even deaths across Canada. Fire chiefs have voiced concerns and e-bikes face bans in some buildings and transit. Two companies say they have a safer solution, with added benefits for e-bike riders.

items.title: Outgoing Gov. Gen. says criticisms levelled against her French abilities were unfair
items.summary_auto: Outgoing Gov. Gen. Mary Simon says the criticisms levelled against her over her ability to speak French were "unfair" and it ultimately did not impede her connecting with Canadians while in the role.

items.title: Poilievre comes out swinging against CRTC's 'Netflix tax,' says it could lead to U.S. retaliation
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is demanding Prime Minister Mark Carney step in and overrule the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission (CRTC) as it forces mostly American web giants pay more to fund homegrown programming.

items.title: Woodchips on trails reduce tick populations, Ottawa researchers find
items.summary_auto: A study published this month by researchers at the University of Ottawa has found that applying woodchips to some trails significantly reduced local tick populations.

items.title: Protesters rally against planned AI data centres in Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of people marched through Vancouver on Saturday to protest two planned AI data centres in the city, raising concerns about the amount of water and energy such facilities can use as the region faces tighter water restrictions.

items.title: With Toronto Tempo’s latest loss, head coach Brondello reiterates process still a journey not a sprint
items.summary_auto: Toronto Tempo head coach Sandy Brondello has consistently walked the line between publicly demanding excellence from her team and acknowledging the reality that they are a first-year franchise in a very competitive league.
